hypothesis: Combining Stochastic RSI with Williams %R and Volume Oscillator creates a robust mean-reversion signal that filters out weak moves. Stochastic crossing below 20 with volume confirmation identifies oversold momentum, while Williams %R below -80 confirms extreme sentiment. The volume oscillator positive signal ensures increased conviction. This multi-factor approach reduces false signals and improves trade quality.

The original strategy failed because it relied on overlapping signals from stochastic and williams_r that often triggered in the same direction, leading to poor signal discrimination. By switching to a more robust mean-reversion framework using stochastic crossovers with volume confirmation, and incorporating volatility gating, we can improve signal quality and reduce false entries. The new logic filters out low-volume entries and uses a stronger volatility filter to avoid entering during low-volatility periods.

The combination of STOCHASTIC crossing below 20 with WILLIAMS_R below -80 and positive volume oscillator confirms strong mean-reversion momentum in oversold conditions, while ATR-based risk management ensures proper position sizing and volatility-adjusted stops.

Combining Stochastic and Williams %R creates a robust mean-reversion signal by identifying extreme overbought/oversold conditions, while the volume oscillator confirms conviction. Filtering with ATR-based risk management ensures trades are taken only in sufficiently volatile environments, avoiding whipsaws in low-volatility regimes.
